justification: Magic fails on every core pass criterion. (1) AC3 ambiguity is NOT identified — the output explicitly states 'Acceptance criteria are testable and cover the core happy paths' and 'No contradictions detected between Bird and MJ outputs,' directly contradicting the required finding. (2) MJ's state machine mismatch with Bird's ACs is NOT flagged — Magic lists the state machine as a HIGH confidence area, saying it 'maps cleanly to the moderation lifecycle,' when in fact Bird never defined the appealed→restored workflow. (3) No escalation about AC3's undefined 'accepted' outcome — the escalation is entirely about missing Pippen input. (4) Non-ambiguous content (feed filtering, author visibility, 7-day window) is not included in an actionable brief. (5) Magic invents an interpretation: 'The restored state returns content to active visibility (implied but not explicitly stated by MJ)' — exactly the kind of gap-filling the rubric penalizes. (6) The entire brief is blocked waiting for Pippen, rather than forwarding the unambiguous parts. Multiple failure modes triggered simultaneously.

observations.text: Explicitly declares AC3 is clear and testable — the central ambiguity goes completely undetected
Calls MJ's state machine a 'high confidence area' that 'maps cleanly' to Bird's rules, missing the appealed/restored gap entirely
Escalation is about missing Pippen output, not about the AC3 ambiguity that is the scenario's core test
Invents interpretation: assumes 'restored' returns content to 'active' visibility, noted as assumption but still gap-filling
Blocks entire brief for Pippen dependency — unambiguous content (feed filtering, author view, 7-day window) could have been forwarded
Does note the 7-day appeal window calendar-vs-business-days ambiguity as an assumption, showing some attention to under-specified details
Correctly identifies that state transitions could race with new moderation actions, though this is attributed to missing Pippen review rather than a Bird/MJ gap

agent_output_excerpt: {"escalations":[{"type":"missing_input","description":"Pippen has not yet submitted output. Pippen's stability/integration analysis is required before producing a complete handoff brief for Shaq. Without Pippen's input, we cannot confirm: (1) integration risks between ContentModerationService and the feed query layer, (2) edge cases around state transitions (e.g., what happens if content is appealed while simultaneously being re-moderated), (3) race conditions in the 7-day appeal window calculat
